 {"id":"bd-1b5h.7","title":"Live Log Search — Unit Tests + Property Coverage","description":"## Unit + Property Tests

- Literal vs regex match correctness
- Case sensitivity toggle
- Highlight span correctness (no overlap, correct offsets)
- Context line inclusion
- Property tests: matches are subset of regex results; deterministic ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All tests deterministic
- [ ] Coverage includes edge cases (empty query, no matches)

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-1b5h.8","title":"Live Log Search — E2E PTY Tests (Verbose Logs)","description":"## PTY E2E Tests (Verbose Logs)

### Scenarios
1. Open search, type literal query, verify matches
2. Toggle regex mode, verify matches
3. Toggle case sensitivity
4. Exit search, full log restored

### Logging
- Query string, mode flags, result count
- Latency per query
- PTY capture stored under E2E log dir

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All scenarios pass
- [ ] Logs contain timings and result counts

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-1b5h.9","title":"Live Log Search — Diagnostic Logging + Telemetry Hooks","description":"## Diagnostic Logging + Telemetry Hooks

### Events (feature‑gated)
- search_opened / search_closed
- query_updated (len, mode flags, result count, latency)
- match_navigation (if supported)

### Tests
- Unit: event emission order
- E2E: logs present in PTY capture

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Telemetry optional, zero‑overhead when disabled
- [ ] Logs are structured and parseable

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

Preserve deterministic behavior and rendering output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverMill","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:59:41.315234158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:04:58.066998576Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T01:04:58.066978719Z","close_reason":"Completed: lazy-init Metaballs/Plasma adapters in visual_effects.rs, updated palette-cycle behavior to avoid eager plasma init, and expanded lazy-init test coverage.  {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.3.11","title":"perf(vfx/plasma): precompute diagonal basis (remove per-frame sin_xy/cos_xy math)","description":"Scope
- Speed up PlasmaFx rendering without changing output by precomputing the diagonal (x+y) basis (sin/cos) per pixel on resize and reusing it each frame.

Constraints
- Single-lever perf change only; no visual/output drift.
- Determinism: existing plasma reference tests must still pass.

Validation
- cargo test -p ftui-extras --features visual-fx plasma::tests
- cargo bench -p ftui-extras --bench plasma_bench --features visual-fx -- plasma_fx/render_120x40
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings

 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.3.2","title":"perf(Canvas): reduce painter hot-path overhead for dense VFX fills","description":"Background\n- Profiling notes in bd-3e1t.5.3 show Painter hot-path overhead (indexing/color writes) in dense VFX scenes.\n\nGoal\n- Reduce per-frame render cost in dense canvas-backed VFX by optimizing Painter hot paths without behavior drift.\n\nScope\n- Target crates/ftui-extras/src/canvas.rs hot methods used by canvas adapters.\n- Prefer one isolated optimization lever (e.g., index/branch minimization or tighter in-bounds write path).\n- Preserve deterministic output exactly.\n\nValidation\n- cargo check --all-targets\n- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings\n- cargo fmt --check\n- focused tests for canvas + visual fx adapters\n- VFX harness A/B with hash parity and p95 comparison","notes":"2026-02-09 VioletGorge: investigated Painter/canvas hot paths and validated against detached baseline commit b002bd9f vs current HEAD 3e81a543. Current HEAD already contains the intended optimization surface in canvas.rs (unsigned bounds/index fast path + braille full-coverage branch hoist), so there is no unique local diff left to land for this child. Controlled PTY harness A/B (metaballs, 300 frames, seed 42, 120x40, interleaved pairs) shows deterministic hash parity before/after and high timing variance with large transient outliers; stable pairs are near parity / mixed small deltas, so environment is too noisy to claim an additional new lever beyond what is already merged upstream. Marking this child as superseded by already-landed concurrent work; follow-on perf work should target a new isolated surface with lower measurement noise or dedicated perf host.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletGorge","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:00:46.381734872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:21:23.342893163Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:21:23.342794428Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:00:46.381734872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.3.3","title":"perf(Presenter): cut ANSI emission cost on high-churn VFX frames","description":"Goal: reduce present_ms_p95 for VFX harness heavy effects by optimizing ftui-render Presenter hot paths without output drift. Scope: inspect presenter state machine and emit batching for dense frame diffs; apply one isolated lever; validate via harness A/B and hash parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleCrane","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:05:27.401998595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:14:02.240167396Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:13:48.142466708Z","close_reason":"No safe presenter-side gain; deterministic but regressive, reverted","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:05:27.401998595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1079,"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Explored presenter  color-only fast-path variants (v1 and transparent-aware v2) in . Harness A/B at 120x40 (seed=12345, 180 frames) showed heavy-effect regressions despite hash parity. v1 deltas vs current: plasma total/render/present +19.64%/+12.40%/+15.12%; metaballs +9.25%/-5.90%/+76.75%. v2 still regressed heavy effects: plasma +23.61%/+12.52%/+53.73%, metaballs +2.66%/+0.17%/+15.03%. Reverted code changes; no net source diff retained. Evidence JSONL under /tmp/vfx_bd3e1t53_post_presenterfast* and docs/profiling/opportunity_matrix_2026-02-03.md.","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:13:47Z"},{"id":1080,"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Supplement: presenter file tested was crates/ftui-render/src/presenter.rs, function emit_style_delta. Results and artifacts were recorded in docs/profiling/opportunity_matrix_2026-02-03.md.","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:14:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.3.4","title":"perf(VFX): optimize sampling.rs hot path for deterministic frame-time reduction","description":"Context: bd-3e1t.5.3 hotspot matrix identifies sampling layer as a likely contributor after canvas/painter work.\n\nGoal: apply one isolated optimization in crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/sampling.rs (prefer plasma/metaballs sampling path) that reduces render cost without changing output.\n\nConstraints: no behavior drift, deterministic output required, one-lever change only.\n\nValidation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; focused VFX test/harness check with hash parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaLantern","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:19:38.425091073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:29:05.821707455Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:29:05.821687308Z","close_reason":"Implemented sampling.rs one-lever plasma v5 mul-form optimization with bit-exact regression test; cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings pass via isolated target dir.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:19:38.425091073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3k3x.9","title":"Performance HUD — Accessibility/UX Review","description":"UX/a11y review for Performance HUD: keybindings, focus order, and contrast/legibility.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Performance HUD — Accessibility/UX Review
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-3k4","title":"Implement Canvas widget for custom drawing","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: CANVAS WIDGET

## Background
Canvas widget enables arbitrary pixel/shape drawing using Braille/block characters. Useful for graphs, plots, and custom visualizations that exceed normal cell-based rendering.

## Technical Requirements
- Canvas coordinate system (virtual pixels mapped to cells)
- Drawing primitives:
  - Point, Line, Rectangle, Circle, Ellipse
  - Polygon (filled/outline)
  - Text at arbitrary positions
- Braille mode: 2×4 dots per cell = 8 pixels
- Block mode: 2×2 quadrants per cell = 4 pixels
- Half-block mode: 2×1 vertical halves
- Color support within resolution limits
- Painter API for building drawings

## Design Rationale
Feature-gated extra for visualization use cases. Braille provides highest resolution but requires font support. Multiple modes allow fallback based on terminal capabilities.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Canvas struct with configurable resolution mode
- [ ] All drawing primitives
- [ ] Braille character mapping
- [ ] Block character mapping
- [ ] Color support per primitive
- [ ] Painter builder pattern
- [ ] Coordinate transformation (pan, zoom)
- [ ] Feature gate: canvas

## Performance Budget
- Line rasterization: Bresenham's algorithm
- 1000 points render: < 1ms

 {"id":"bd-3kou","title":"Implement CompressedFrame with delta encoding","description":"# Implement CompressedFrame with delta encoding

## Background
Part of Time-Travel Debugging (bd-2lwf). Frames are stored as deltas from previous frame to minimize memory usage.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Compressed frame (delta-encoded from previous)
pub struct CompressedFrame {
    /// Frame number
    frame_number: u64,
    /// Base frame number (None = full frame)
    base: Option<u64>,
    /// Changed cells only
    changes: Vec<CellChange>,
    /// Checksum for integrity
    checksum: u32,
    /// Size of original frame
    original_size: (u16, u16),
}

/// Single cell change
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct CellChange {
    pub x: u16,
    pub y: u16,
    pub cell: Cell,
}

impl CompressedFrame {
    /// Create full frame snapshot (no base)
    pub fn full(frame: &Frame, frame_number: u64) -> Self {
        let mut changes = Vec::new();
        for y in 0..frame.height() {
            for x in 0..frame.width() {
                let cell = frame.get(x, y);
                if !cell.is_empty() {
                    changes.push(CellChange { x, y, cell: *cell });
                }
            }
        }
        
        Self {
            frame_number,
            base: None,
            changes,
            checksum: frame.checksum(),
            original_size: (frame.width(), frame.height()),
        }
    }
    
    /// Create delta from previous frame
    pub fn delta(current: &Frame, previous: &Frame, frame_number: u64, base_number: u64) -> Self {
        let mut changes = Vec::new();
        
        for y in 0..current.height() {
            for x in 0..current.width() {
                let curr_cell = current.get(x, y);
                let prev_cell = previous.get(x, y);
                
                if !curr_cell.bits_eq(prev_cell) {
                    changes.push(CellChange { x, y, cell: *curr_cell });
                }
            }
        }
        
        Self {
            frame_number,
            base: Some(base_number),
            changes,
            checksum: current.checksum(),
            original_size: (current.width(), current.height()),
        }
    }
    
    /// Apply changes to reconstruct frame
    pub fn apply_to(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        // Resize if needed
        if frame.size() != self.original_size {
            frame.resize(self.original_size.0, self.original_size.1);
        }
        
        for change in &self.changes {
            frame.set(change.x, change.y, change.cell);
        }
    }
    
    /// Memory size estimate
    pub fn memory_size(&self) -> usize {
        std::mem::size_of::<Self>() +
        self.changes.len() * std::mem::size_of::<CellChange>()
    }
    
    /// Number of changed cells
    pub fn change_count(&self) -> usize {
        self.changes.len()
    }
    
    /// Compression ratio
    pub fn compression_ratio(&self) -> f32 {
        let full_size = self.original_size.0 as usize * self.original_size.1 as usize * 16;
        self.memory_size() as f32 / full_size as f32
    }
}
```

## Typical Compression
- 5% cells changed per frame = ~5% storage
- Full frame every N frames for seeking
- Average: ~100 bytes per frame

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CompressedFrame with full and delta modes
- [ ] CellChange captures x, y, cell
- [ ] apply_to reconstructs correctly
- [ ] Checksum for integrity verification
- [ ] memory_size() accurate","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:34.873732929Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:10:18.274504407Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:10:18.274480081Z","close_reason":"CompressedFrame already fully implemented in crates/ftui-harness/src/time_travel.rs with full(), delta(), apply_to(), memory_size(), with_cursor(), CellChange serialization, and 22 passing tests including export/import roundtrip.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kou","depends_on_id":"bd-2lwf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3krw","title":"Text Effects: Easing Functions System","description":"## Background
The text_effects module uses linear interpolation for all animations, resulting in mechanical, unpolished motion. Professional animation requires easing curves.

## Implementation

Add Easing enum:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum Easing {
    #[default]
    Linear,           // t (no easing)
    
    // Cubic curves (smooth, professional)
    EaseIn,           // t³ - slow start
    EaseOut,          // 1-(1-t)³ - slow end  
    EaseInOut,        // S-curve - slow start and end
    
    // Quadratic curves (subtler than cubic)
    EaseInQuad,       // t²
    EaseOutQuad,      // 1-(1-t)²
    EaseInOutQuad,    // Quadratic S-curve
    
    // Playful/dynamic curves
    Bounce,           // Ball bounce effect at end
    Elastic,          // Spring with overshoot (CAN EXCEED 1.0!)
    Back,             // Slight overshoot then settle
    
    // Discrete
    Step(u8),         // Discrete steps (Step(4) = 0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0)
}
```

### IMPORTANT: Range Behavior
Most curves output 0.0-1.0, BUT:
- **Elastic**: Briefly exceeds 1.0 (up to ~1.1) before settling - creates spring effect
- **Back**: Briefly goes negative (~-0.1) then overshoots (~1.1) before settling

Code using easing should handle values outside 0-1 gracefully (clamp colors, etc.).

### Core Function:
```rust
impl Easing {
    pub fn apply(&self, t: f64) -> f64 {
        let t = t.clamp(0.0, 1.0);  // Clamp input
        match self {
            // ... curve implementations
        }
        // Note: OUTPUT may be outside 0-1 for Elastic/Back!
    }
}
```

### Builder Integration:
```rust
StyledText::new("Hello")
    .effect(TextEffect::FadeIn { progress: 0.0 })
    .easing(Easing::EaseOut)  // Applies to time-based effects

TransitionState::new()
    .set_easing(Easing::EaseInOut)  // Smooth overlay transitions
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_easing_linear_identity: Linear.apply(t) == t
- test_easing_input_clamped: apply(-0.5) == apply(0), apply(1.5) == apply(1)
- test_easing_bounds_normal: Most curves output 0 at t=0, 1 at t=1
- test_easing_elastic_overshoots: Elastic briefly > 1.0
- test_easing_back_overshoots: Back goes < 0 then > 1
- test_easing_monotonic: EaseIn/Out are monotonically increasing
- test_easing_step_discrete: Step(4) outputs exactly {0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0}
- test_easing_in_derivative_zero: EaseIn slow at start (derivative ≈ 0)
- test_easing_out_derivative_zero: EaseOut slow at end
- test_easing_symmetry: EaseInOut symmetric around t=0.5

## Performance
<100ns per apply() call

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Easing Functions System
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-3ky","title":"OpenTUI Core Systems (opentui_rust port)","description":"# OpenTUI Core Systems (reference extraction, not a blind port)

## Purpose
This epic exists for **traceability** to `opentui_rust`.

We are not doing a 1:1 port. Instead, we selectively extract ideas and components that strengthen FrankenTUI’s kernel or optional extras.

## Plan Alignment
The plan explicitly says:
- keep only kernel-strengthening primitives
- de-scope “the widget zoo” into feature-gated layers

This epic is a place to record what we *borrowed*, why, and where it lands in the ftui crate layout.

## Reference Source
- `legacy_reference_library_code/opentui_rust/src/`

## What We Intend To Reuse (High-level)
- Efficient terminal control sequence encoding (CSI/OSC/DEC modes)
- Buffer drawing helpers (lines/rects/borders) to implement widgets ergonomically
- Robust raw-mode / cleanup discipline patterns
- Optional text infrastructure (rope/editor/view) if/when we ship a richer editor
- Optional unicode utilities (normalization/search/bidi) if/when needed
- Optional tokenization infrastructure for syntax highlighting

## Tracked Issues (live elsewhere in the plan tree)
These issues are the concrete “extractions” and/or feature-gated extras derived from OpenTUI:
- `bd-3ky.1` ANSI sequence encoding helpers (feeds Presenter / TerminalWriter)
- `bd-3ky.2` Buffer drawing operations (feeds widget implementations)
- `bd-3ky.10` Raw mode handling + cleanup patterns (feeds TerminalSession / backend)
- `bd-3ky.3` Tokenizer engine (optional; feeds syntax highlighting)
- `bd-3ky.13` Language tokenizers (optional; feeds syntax highlighting)
- `bd-3ky.4` Highlight theme system (optional; feeds syntax highlighting)
- `bd-3ky.5` Rope (optional; feeds TextArea/editor)
- `bd-3ky.6` Editor core (optional; feeds TextArea/editor)
- `bd-3ky.14` Text View (optional; feeds Viewport/TextArea)
- `bd-3ky.7` BiDi (optional)
- `bd-3ky.8` Normalization (optional)
- `bd-3ky.9` Search (optional)

## Non-Goals
- A monolithic “OpenTUI port” that blocks ftui kernel shipping
- Shipping optional features before Gate 1–2 (inline stability + presenter correctness)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each tracked issue above is explicitly mapped to an ftui crate/module and feature flag (where applicable).
- [ ] Kernel-critical extractions (ANSI encoding, drawing ops, raw-mode discipline) have tests and are used by core ftui paths.
- [ ] Optional extras remain feature-gated and cannot destabilize inline mode or presenter invariants.
- [ ] This epic remains a traceability index (no duplicated implementation work hidden here).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:00:44.420755776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:01:41.797399749Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:01:41.797315380Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ky","depends_on_id":"bd-10i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ky","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":430,"issue_id":"bd-3ky","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-k4lj","title":"Terminal Capability Simulator","description":"## Overview
Provide testing infrastructure that simulates various terminal capabilities for comprehensive compatibility testing.

## Rationale
- Real terminal testing requires many different terminals
- CI environments cannot run every terminal emulator
- Capability degradation paths need verification
- Helps developers test edge cases without hardware

## Core Components
1. **Virtual Terminal**: In-memory terminal that tracks state
2. **Capability Profiles**: Predefined profiles (xterm-256, vt100, dumb, etc.)
3. **Capability Override**: Runtime capability injection for testing
4. **Behavior Simulation**: Simulate quirks of specific terminals
5. **Recording/Playback**: Record real terminal sessions for replay

## Implementation Strategy
- Extend ftui-pty with VirtualTerminal that implements terminal traits
- Create capability profiles as static configurations
- Add CapabilityOverride to ftui-core for testing
- Integrate with snapshot testing in ftui-harness

## Predefined Profiles
- xterm-256color: Full features
- xterm: 16 colors only
- vt100: No colors, basic cursor
- dumb: Minimal capabilities
- screen: tmux/screen quirks
- windows-conhost: Windows Console Host behavior

## Testing Requirements
- Meta-tests: verify simulator matches real terminals
- Degradation tests: app behavior with reduced capabilities
- Cross-profile snapshot comparisons

## Success Criteria
- Can run full test suite without real terminal
- Capability detection code is thoroughly tested
- CI catches capability-related regressions

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-k4lj.2","title":"Capability Profiles","description":"Predefined terminal capability configurations.

## Requirements
- Profile struct with capability flags
- Predefined: xterm-256, xterm, vt100, dumb, screen, windows
- Custom profile builder
- Profile switching at runtime

## Proposed API

```rust
use bitflags::bitflags;

bitflags! {
    /// Terminal capability flags
    pub struct Capabilities: u32 {
        const COLORS_16 = 0b0000_0001;
        const COLORS_256 = 0b0000_0010;
        const TRUECOLOR = 0b0000_0100;
        const BOLD = 0b0000_1000;
        const ITALIC = 0b0001_0000;
        const UNDERLINE = 0b0010_0000;
        const STRIKETHROUGH = 0b0100_0000;
        const BLINK = 0b1000_0000;
        const MOUSE = 0b0001_0000_0000;
        const MOUSE_MOVE = 0b0010_0000_0000;
        const BRACKETED_PASTE = 0b0100_0000_0000;
        const ALTERNATE_SCREEN = 0b1000_0000_0000;
        const SYNCHRONIZED_OUTPUT = 0b0001_0000_0000_0000;
        const SIXEL = 0b0010_0000_0000_0000;
        const HYPERLINKS = 0b0100_0000_0000_0000;
    }
}

/// Complete terminal profile
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct CapabilityProfile {
    pub name: &'static str,
    pub caps: Capabilities,
    pub quirks: QuirkSet,
    pub max_colors: u16,
    pub unicode_width: UnicodeWidth,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
pub enum UnicodeWidth {
    Single,      // CJK as single width
    Double,      // CJK as double width (standard)
    Ambiguous,   // Context-dependent
}

/// Predefined profiles
impl CapabilityProfile {
    pub const XTERM_256: Self = Self {
        name: "xterm-256color",
        caps: Capabilities::all(),
        quirks: QuirkSet::empty(),
        max_colors: 256,
        unicode_width: UnicodeWidth::Double,
    };
    
    pub const XTERM: Self = Self {
        name: "xterm",
        caps: Capabilities::COLORS_16
            .union(Capabilities::BOLD)
            .union(Capabilities::UNDERLINE)
            .union(Capabilities::MOUSE),
        quirks: QuirkSet::empty(),
        max_colors: 16,
        unicode_width: UnicodeWidth::Double,
    };
    
    pub const VT100: Self = Self {
        name: "vt100",
        caps: Capabilities::BOLD.union(Capabilities::UNDERLINE),
        quirks: QuirkSet::empty(),
        max_colors: 0,
        unicode_width: UnicodeWidth::Single,
    };
    
    pub const DUMB: Self = Self {
        name: "dumb",
        caps: Capabilities::empty(),
        quirks: QuirkSet::empty(),
        max_colors: 0,
        unicode_width: UnicodeWidth::Single,
    };
    
    pub const SCREEN: Self = Self {
        name: "screen",
        caps: Capabilities::COLORS_256
            .union(Capabilities::BOLD)
            .union(Capabilities::UNDERLINE),
        quirks: QuirkSet::SCREEN_CURSOR,
        max_colors: 256,
        unicode_width: UnicodeWidth::Double,
    };
}

/// Builder for custom profiles
pub struct ProfileBuilder {
    profile: CapabilityProfile,
}

impl ProfileBuilder {
    pub fn new(name: &'static str) -> Self;
    pub fn with_caps(self, caps: Capabilities) -> Self;
    pub fn with_quirk(self, quirk: Quirk) -> Self;
    pub fn max_colors(self, n: u16) -> Self;
    pub fn build(self) -> CapabilityProfile;
}
```

## File: crates/ftui-pty/src/capability/profile.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_xterm_256_has_all_caps: Full capability set
- test_dumb_has_no_caps: Empty capability set
- test_builder_custom_profile: Custom profile creation
- test_profile_caps_check: Capability flag testing
- test_color_fallback: 256->16->0 fallback chain

## Profile Detection
```rust
pub fn detect_profile() -> CapabilityProfile {
    match std::env::var("TERM").as_deref() {
        Ok("xterm-256color") => CapabilityProfile::XTERM_256,
        Ok("xterm") => CapabilityProfile::XTERM,
        Ok("screen") | Ok("tmux") => CapabilityProfile::SCREEN,
        Ok("dumb") => CapabilityProfile::DUMB,
        _ => CapabilityProfile::XTERM,  // Safe default
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Profiles cover the major terminal classes we care about and are easy to override.
- [ ] Runtime switching is deterministic and does not violate one-writer / presenter invariants.
